description: A GET /contexts/search request supplies a cursor the registry cannot parse at all — a malformed, truncated, or fabricated opaque string that was never issued by this registry. The registry MUST respond with invalid_cursor (HTTP 400) and the standard error envelope. See RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.5.4 'Cursor stability'.

input.scenario: The cursor value is garbage — supplied by a buggy client or a malicious client probing the pagination surface. It does not decode to any registry pagination state.

expected.rationale: invalid_cursor (never parseable) is distinct from cursor_expired (was valid, now stale — see cur-001). A registry MUST NOT leak why a cursor failed to parse beyond the registered code, and MUST NOT attempt to act on a partially-decoded cursor. Cursors are opaque and MUST NOT carry client-decodable visibility state (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.5.4); a malformed cursor MUST be rejected outright, never best-effort interpreted.
